---
title: "Deep Dive: Shinylive (Serverless Apps)"
---

## What is Shinylive?
 **Shinylive** allows for interactive Shiny applications to run entirely **serverless** inside a regular web browser.

## How it Works: WebAssembly (Wasm)
Shinylive influence **WebAssembly (Wasm)** to compile the R programming language time into instructions that a browser can read and manage promptly. When a user loads the site, the lightweight R environment is cached right in the browser, which means all reactive computations happen organically on their computer.

## Comparison Table

| Feature | Traditional Shiny | Shinylive |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Hosting Needs** | Dedicated R Server | Static web hosting (GitHub Pages, Quarto-Pub) |
| **Computation Location** | Cloud / Remote Server | User's local browser |
| **Cost & Scalability** | Paid plans required for heavy usage | Completely free; infinitely scalable |
| **Initial Load Time** | Instant | Slightly slower (loads R runtime first) |

## Pros and Cons of Shinylive

### Pros
* **Zero Hosting Costs:** Deploy full apps easily on GitHub Pages.
* **Infinite Scale:** The host site will never crash from high traffic as every user's device pulls its own weight.
* **Data Security:** Data processing remains only inside the user's browser without having to transfer to an external server.

### Cons
* **Initial Download Overhead:** The initial page load takes a little longer to capture the runtime of the browser-side R.
* **Hardware Constraints:** Complex calculations can be limitted by the speed of the user's personal device rather than the speed of the cloud servers.
